STATEMENT TO THE  (NGX) AND SHAREHOLDERS
ON THE
UNAUDITED FINANCIAL RESULTS FOR THE QUARTER ENDED 31 MARCH 2026

The Directors of Guinness Nigeria Plc hereby announce the Company's unaudited results for the quarter ended 31 March 2026.

Commentaries
Guinness Nigeria Plc recorded a Profit After Tax of NGN10.39 billion for the quarter ended 31 March 2026, compared to NGN7.03 billion in the same quarter of the prior year. The performance reflects the Company's continued resilience and disciplined execution in a challenging operating environment.

Key Financial Highlights
Revenue: Revenue for Q1 FY2026 was NGN122.77 billion, compared to NGN118.34 billion in Q1 FY2025, reflecting growth of approximately 4% year-on-year.
Gross Profit: Gross profit was NGN43.48 billion, representing a gross margin of 35.4%, compared to NGN44.52 billion and a margin of 37.6% in Q1 FY2025. The margin movement reflects prevailing cost pressures within the operating environment.
Operating Profit: Profit from operating activities was NGN17.18 billion for the quarter, compared to NGN17.99 billion in Q1 FY2025.


Guinness Nigeria Headquarters, Cocoa Industries Road (Guinness Road), Ogba, Ikeja Lagos| Telephone +234 0201 2709100 | www.guinness-nigeria.com | RC 771

Directors: Prof. Fabian Ajogwu, SAN (Chairman) | Mr. Harkishin Aswani (Singaporean) (Vice Chair) | Mr. Girish Sharma (Indian) (Managing Director) | Mr. Mayank
Kabra (Indian) (Finance & Strategy Director) | Mrs. Olusola Oworu | Mr. Deepak Singhal (Indian) | Mrs. Vivien Shobo  | Mr. John Musunga (Kenyan) | Mrs. Mrs Bola
Adesola
Finance Costs: Net finance costs reduced to NGN1.43 billion in Q1 FY2026 compared to NGN7.72 billion in Q1
FY2025, reflecting improved Balance Sheet efficiency and reduced financing costs.
Profit After Tax: Profit after tax was NGN10.39 billion for the quarter (Q1 FY2025: NGN7.03 billion), representing earnings per share of NGN4.74 (Q1 FY2025: NGN3.21).

Interim Dividend
Following the sustained profitability reflected in the Q1 FY2026 results and the Company's strong performance in FY2025, the Board of Directors has approved an interim dividend of NGN2.00 per ordinary share, with a qualification date of 20 April 2026. The interim dividend represents a total payout of approximately NGN4.38 billion, based on 2,190,382,819 ordinary shares in issue. The dividend will be paid from distributable profits in accordance with Sections 426-428 of the Companies and Allied Matters Act (CAMA) 2020.



By Order of the Board.
Abimbola Ajibola-Jimoh
Company Secretary
